{{Shrine |name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (都祁山口神社) |image = {{Image requested|cat=Tenri City}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}} Soma-no-Uchi Town 896 |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 31 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 52 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |map_country = JP |enshrined_deities = * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (main deity) * {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} * {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} |divine_body = |shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) debated |established = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with cypress bark roofing |other_names = |temple_tags = |main_festival = {{ill|October 14|ja|10月14日}} |rituals = |map = Japan Nara }}
'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} and {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} as additional deities.
== History == === Overview === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).
=== Shrine Rank === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}}

== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} and {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} as additional deities.
== History == === Overview === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).

'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} and {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} as additional deities.
== History == === Overview === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).
=== Shrine Rank === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}}

The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.

The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.

'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} and {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} as additional deities.
== History == === Overview === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.

=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.

'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} and {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} as additional deities.
== History == === Overview === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.

{{Shrine |name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (都祁山口神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Tenri City}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}} Soma-no-Uchi Town 896 |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 31 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 52 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |map_country = JP |enshrined_deities = * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (main deity) * {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} * {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} |divine_body = |shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) debated |established = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with cypress bark roofing |other_names = |temple_tags = |main_festival = {{ill|October 14|ja|10月14日}} |rituals = |map = Japan Nara }}
'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} and {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} as additional deities.
== History == === Overview === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.

{{Shrine |name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (都祁山口神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Tenri City}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}} Soma-no-Uchi Town 896 |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 31 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 52 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |map_country = JP |enshrined_deities = * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (main deity) * {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} * {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} |divine_body = |shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) debated |established = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with cypress bark roofing |other_names = |temple_tags = |main_festival = {{ill|October 14|ja|10月14日}} |rituals = |map = Japan Nara }}
'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} and {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} as additional deities.
== History == === Overview === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}}

{{Shrine |name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (都祁山口神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Tenri City}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}} Soma-no-Uchi Town 896 |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 31 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 52 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |map_country = JP |enshrined_deities = * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (main deity) * {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} * {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} |divine_body = |shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) debated |established = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with cypress bark roofing |other_names = |temple_tags = |main_festival = {{ill|October 14|ja|10月14日}} |rituals = |map = Japan Nara }}
'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} and {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} as additional deities.
== History == === Overview === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.

{{Shrine |name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (都祁山口神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Tenri City}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}} Soma-no-Uchi Town 896 |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 31 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 52 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |map_country = JP |enshrined_deities = * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (main deity) * {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} * {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} |divine_body = |shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) debated |established = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with cypress bark roofing |other_names = |temple_tags = |main_festival = {{ill|October 14|ja|10月14日}} |rituals = |map = Japan Nara }}
'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} and {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} as additional deities.
== History == === Overview === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (都祁山口神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Tenri City}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}} Soma-no-Uchi Town 896 |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 31 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 52 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |map_country = JP |enshrined_deities = * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (main deity) * {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} * {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} |divine_body = |shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) debated |established = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} with cypress bark roofing |other_names = |temple_tags = |main_festival = {{ill|October 14|ja|10月14日}} |rituals = |map = Japan Nara }}
'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} and {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} as additional deities.
== History == === Overview === The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}}

== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text.== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}}

'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命}} and {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40th year }} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsugeyamaguchi Shrine]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (天理市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q59282581]] Wikidata name: Tsugeyamaguchi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:31, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (都祁山口神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsugeyamaguchi Shrine in Tenri.jpg |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]] Soma-no-Uchi Town 896 |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 31 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 52 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |map_country = JP |enshrined_deities = * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (main deity) * {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |divine_body = |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) debated |established = Unknown |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with cypress bark roofing |other_names = |temple_tags = |main_festival = [[:en:October 14|October 14]] |rituals = |map = Japan Nara |location = {{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.591944|N|135.847778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.

'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]]) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]]) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]]) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsugeyamaguchi Shrine]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (天理市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q59282581]] Wikidata name: Tsugeyamaguchi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:31, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (都祁山口神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsugeyamaguchi Shrine in Tenri.jpg |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]] Soma-no-Uchi Town 896 |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 31 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 52 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |map_country = JP |enshrined_deities = * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (main deity) * {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |divine_body = |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) debated |established = Unknown |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with cypress bark roofing |other_names = |temple_tags = |main_festival = [[:en:October 14|October 14]] |rituals = |map = Japan Nara |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.591944|N|135.847778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]]) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]]) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsugeyamaguchi Shrine]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (天理市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q59282581]] Wikidata name: Tsugeyamaguchi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:31, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (都祁山口神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsugeyamaguchi Shrine in Tenri.jpg |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]] Soma-no-Uchi Town 896 |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 31 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 52 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |map_country = JP |enshrined_deities = * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (main deity) * {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |divine_body = |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) debated |established = Unknown |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with cypress bark roofing |other_names = |temple_tags = |main_festival = [[:en:October 14|October 14]] |rituals = |map = Japan Nara |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.591944|N|135.847778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]]) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]]) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]]) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (都祁山口神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsugeyamaguchi Shrine in Tenri.jpg |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}} Soma-no-Uchi Town 896 |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 31 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 52 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |map_country = JP |enshrined_deities = * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (main deity) * {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |divine_body = |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) debated |established = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} with cypress bark roofing |other_names = |temple_tags = |main_festival = {{ill|October 14|en|October 14|WD=Q2922|lt=October 14}} |rituals = |map = Japan Nara |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.591944|N|135.847778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji|1=Jingū-ji}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (都祁山口神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsugeyamaguchi Shrine in Tenri.jpg |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}} Soma-no-Uchi Town 896 |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 31 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 52 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |map_country = JP |enshrined_deities = * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (main deity) * {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |divine_body = |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) debated |established = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with cypress bark roofing |other_names = |temple_tags = |main_festival = {{ill|October 14|en|October 14|WD=Q2922|lt=October 14|1=October 14}} |rituals = |map = Japan Nara |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.591944|N|135.847778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and is considered one of the fourteen "Yamaguchi Gods" of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, with {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久迩知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|波爾夜須命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as additional deities.
== History == == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as Mikuchi Myōjin (水口明神) or Hakusan Daigongen (白山大権現) and also worshipped {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} as Wakamiya (young deity).
The shrine's associated temple ({{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji|1=Jingū-ji}}) was abolished in {{ill|Meiji 40|ja|明治40年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 40|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1907年|1=1907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2048}} (1907).
=== type === * {{ill|Ten'an|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} 3rd year (859), January 27: Elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (正五位下) as per the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. The shrine is referred to as "Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine" in the text. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
